news.summary: In Piscataway, New Jersey, a collaboration between IEEE and IEEE SA has come to a conclusion regarding the Indonesian Government Regulation on the Governance of Electronic Systems in Child Protection. This collaboration marks an important milestone in addressing child protection issues in the digital realm.
news.headline: IEEE and IEEE SA collaborate with Indonesia on its first age-appropriate design regulation, enhancing child protection.

news.summary: Mike Cahill, the CEO of Douro Labs, discusses the disparities in access to traditional wealth-building opportunities worldwide. In the US, the top 10% of earners own over 90% of all stocks, while globally, billions lack financial literacy and resources to invest. Cahill emphasizes the need for traditional institutions to leverage digital assets for new use cases, particularly in disrupting equities. He highlights issues in pricing, execution, and settlement processes that limit access to equities, mainly affecting smaller investors. Decentralized finance (DeFi) can address these challenges by offering real-time pricing, fractional equity exposure, and instant settlements through blockchain technology. Cahill calls for the collaboration of DeFi and traditional finance to create a financial system that is faster, more accessible, and transparent, ultimately bridging the wealth gap and democratizing access to equities for the global population.
news.headline: Equities are the facet of traditional finance most ripe for disruption due to limited worldwide access. Top 10% earners in the US own over 90% of stocks. Billions lack the literacy and funds for basic investment. Decentralized tech can enhance equity access by improving pricing, execution, and settlement systems, creating a more inclusive financial market. Decentralized finance offers solutions for these challenges, potentially bridging the wealth gap.

news.headline: Elina Onitskansky innovates obesity care with personalized treatment, AI approach, aiming to address root causes of obesity. TAMPA BAY, Fla.  #ObesityCare #PersonalizedTreatment #AI #TAMPAFL.

news.summary: Global-E Online Ltd. (NASDAQ: GLBE) saw a decrease in its shares after announcing its first-quarter FY25 results. The company experienced a 30% year-over-year sales growth, reaching $189.9 million, surpassing the analyst consensus of $187.87 million. Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) showed a 34% increase year over year, reaching $1.24 billion for the quarter. Service fees amounted to $84.0 million, while fulfillment services revenue stood at $105.9 million. The non-GAAP gross profit also increased to $86.3 million, marking a 31% year-over-year rise, with the non-GAAP gross margin expanding to 45.4%, a 10 basis points increase year over year. Adjusted EBITDA grew to $31.6 million from $21.3 million in the previous year. Moreover, the company narrowed its operating loss from $29.3 million to $19.2 million. The loss per share was $0.11, surpassing the expected loss of $0.13. Looking ahead, for the second quarter, Global-E Online projects GMV... (Remaining information available for viewing on Benzinga.com)
news.headline: Global-e Online Q1 FY25 results: Sales up 30% to $189.9M, GMV rises 34% to $1.24B, non-GAAP gross profit up 31% to $86.3M.
